mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% represents an ultra-low monetary stance. When FEDFUNDS remains below 0.50%, equity risk premiums compress and discount rates fall, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation regime with rates near zero
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment at 7.0% signals labor market slack. When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, firms face lower wage pressure and higher labor supply elasticity, reducing cost inflation and supporting profit margins. This mechanism drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations stabilize.
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with elevated slack
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) when UNRATE > 6.5% versus UNRATE ≤ 6.5%, or mechanism fails to predict direction in > 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 432,310 million dollars combined with housing starts at 883 thousand units indicate sustained consumer demand. When both RSAFS and HOUST remain above their 12-month median, aggregate demand is robust, supporting equity valuations over the next 5 trading days through positive earnings revisions.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer activity
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.15) when both RSAFS and HOUST exceed their medians, or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal by more than 50 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 99.98 (near 2017 baseline) indicates supply-side constraints. When INDPRO falls below 100.0 for two consecutive months, supply tightness relative to demand raises inflation expectations and real discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as growth concerns dominate.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO < 100.0 for 2+ months, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 233.81 combined with FEDFUNDS at 0.08% creates a large negative real rate environment. When real rates (FEDFUNDS minus 12-month CPI growth) fall below -2.0%, equity valuations expand as investors flee cash, driving positive 60-day returns.
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) when real rates < -2.0% versus real rates ≥ -2.0%, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 136,765 thousand persons combined with unemployment at 7.0% indicates labor market deterioration. When PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises above 6.8%, recession signals trigger equity selloffs. This mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as growth expectations collapse.
regime_hint: early-stage labor market deterioration signaling recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal underperforms a simple UNRATE-only model by more than 100 basis points in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 15,819 billion chained 2017 dollars represents the demand foundation. When GDPC1 growth (annualized quarterly change) exceeds 2.5%, the economy operates above trend, supporting equity earnings growth. This always-on mechanism predicts positive 60-day returns during above-trend growth regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.10) during quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% versus ≤ 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 99.98 combined with inflation at 233.81 creates a supply-demand imbalance signal. When INDPRO declines while CPIAUCSL accelerates (month-over-month growth > 0.3%), stagflation risk emerges. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 5-day returns as equity risk premiums widen.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM AND CPIAUCSL MoM growth > 0.3%
regime_hint: stagflation risk regime with supply constraints and price acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.20) in logistic regression predicting return direction.
